description_long: This is a pair of bongo drums connected by a wooden bridge, featuring cylindrical wooden bodies with natural skin drumheads stretched tightly across the tops. The drum shells are a light brown wood grain, reinforced at the top and bottom with black rims and held together by silver metal tuning lugs and brackets. The drumheads appear slightly textured and off-white, showing subtle discoloration from use. The connecting bridge is a simple rectangular wooden piece, joining the two drums at their sides. The overall construction is robust, with visible hardware for tuning and stability. This instrument is commonly used in street performances, parks, or community gatherings in urban environments.
description: A pair of wooden bongo drums with natural skin heads, joined by a wooden bridge and reinforced with black rims and silver hardware. The drums show a light brown wood grain and are designed for musical use in urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing both drumheads, the connecting bridge, and the full set of tuning hardware.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical shape of one drum and the depth of the drum shell.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing both drumheads and the connecting bridge from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the other drum, with visible shell depth and hardware.
